rationale: The candidate studies epidemic spread on social networks using SIR (susceptible/infective/removed) dynamical models, which are compartmental differential equation models of infection transmission, not the same core machinery as the query's static, dynamical, or constructive network models (e.g., rule-based link assignment, population dynamics, or closure-driven growth). The object is genuinely different (social networks of disease transmission vs. food webs or chemical reaction networks).
